source: All-In Podcast — E278: AI memory crunch, Micron blowout, NYC socialists, SpaceX float (w/ Kalanick)
summary: Micron's new supply agreements with floor pricing above prior cycle gross margin peaks are potentially transformational for the DRAM industry
summary_extended: Baker argues that Micron's newly announced supply and demand agreements (SDAs) — covering approximately 50% of Micron's revenue with just four customers and featuring both floor and ceiling pricing — represent a potentially transformational structural shift in the DRAM industry's business model. The critical detail is that the floor pricing embedded in these contracts already exceeds the gross margin peaks achieved during prior DRAM upcycles, meaning Micron's downside in future cycles is structurally protected at levels that previously only existed at the top of the cycle. Baker believes the market has not yet fully priced this structural improvement, implying Micron is undervalued relative to its new earnings floor. The investment implication is to be long MU, as the SDA structure de-risks the cyclicality that has historically caused investors to discount DRAM stocks, and the contracts cover enough revenue (50% with four customers) to materially anchor profitability through downturns. No specific price target or timeframe was cited, but Baker frames this as a medium-term re-rating catalyst as the market comes to appreciate the new margin floor.
detail: Micron announced supply agreements (SDAs) covering ~50% of revenue with just four customers, with floor and ceiling pricing. The floor pricing in these contracts exceeds prior cycle peak gross margins, which Baker views as a structural improvement in the DRAM business model that the market has not fully priced.
confidence_evidence: Baker describes this as 'pretty maybe end up being very transformational for the industry' and provides specific contract details
quote_fragment: floor pricing in these new contracts is ahead of prior cycle peaks from a gross margin perspective

ticker_audit.reasoning: Micron is the explicit subject of this thesis — the SDAs are Micron's contracts, and the structural gross margin improvement is specific to Micron's business model.
